prompt_rules: single large tree object with canopy, trunk, contact shadow, and foreground-overlap potential
draw as one coherent object rather than stacked square tiles
asymmetric canopy, leaf clusters, trunk shadow, and root details should reduce grid feeling

prompt:
Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette: DEEP rich saturated greens (early-summer rice-field banks), clear blue-green water, vermilion and white accents, soft warm sunlight. NOT dry, NOT yellow-brown, NOT a western prairie or wasteland. ONE Japanese red pine tree (akamatsu) as a single coherent pixel-art object: elegant gently S-curved reddish-brown trunk, layered cloud-like pads of deep green needles like a shrine garden pine, soft contact shadow at the roots. Front view, the tree fills most of the canvas with margin. No text.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.
